The Hawks are stingy defensively on lead ball handlers, allowing the fourth fewest points to point guards over the last 15 games, and rank in the top 8 against pick and roll ball- handler points allowed. Against teams in the top 20 of both PnR defense and limiting pull-up shooters (Hawks are 9th best), Nembhard is under in 16/27 games. He’s been more passive lately as is, and the Pacers have Bennedict Mathurin back.

I personally like seeing downtrodden teams finally rise up (just not apparently the Bulls) as the Hornets are doing. What a great pick Kon Knueppel was at No. 4 in the 2025 draft. If you redraft, he probably goes second. White boy with not-great athleticism? I thought he'd struggle in the pros kinda like Steve Alford did; he was a similar player in college. But Kon shoots the lights out and hasn't stopped in the NBA. He's Vanilla Curry (tm). I'm sure the Bulls would have passed on him and taken Dalen Terry or the like. Insert Charlie Brown sigh here. Half unit as the Spurs are excellent.

Agreed, some very good efforts from the Spurs over the past month, but drill a bit deeper and note a very so-so 9-8 SU mark since after the Christmas win over the Thunder, alternating wins and losses the past two weeks. January setbacks to the likes of Portland, Memphis, and New Orleans suggest to be careful picking out proper spots to go with the Spurs, who haven't won back-to-back games on the road since before Christmas.  The Hornets are the hotter of these two teams, leading NBA net rating in January, and with a five-game win streak entering this afternoon after Kon Knueppel's 34 points help see of the Mavs and Kon's old Duke teammate Cooper Flagg (49 points and all) on Thursday. Play Hornets

This is a large line for De’Anthony Melton considering his role, usage, and playing time for the Warriors. It’s also a bit inflated due to having eclipsed this in three consecutive games. He’s playing against one of the top perimeter defenses in the league and this line typically hovers around 10.5 to 11.5.

The Pistons simply had no answers for the Suns last night, but those types of flat spots have rarely endured for JB Bickerstaff's bunch this season. To wit: Detroit has lost consecutive games just twice, and not within the last month, usually bouncing back smartly from a loss. The Pistons have no serious injury concerns at the moment, and last night's result might be a more a case of a tough matchup vs. Phoenix, which also when minus Devin Booker played Detroit tough at Little Caesars on January 15. We still view the current version of the Warriors quite warily, with no Jimmy Butler or Jonathan Kuminga, and losing three of five minus Butler, with Wednesday's win at Utah hardly a buy signal. Play Pistons.

FanDuel. Ace Bailey is starting to find his footing offensively for the Jazz. The rookie has cleared this line in six of his last eight, and has got up at least 15 shots in each of his last five outings. He’ll face a Nets defense that looks to take away lead ball-handlers (Keyonte George in this instance) and is a funnel for spot-up shooters and secondary actions. Bailey should thrive against this defense, especially with Lauri Markkanen out tonight.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Jonas Valanciunas Under 28.5 Total Points + Assists + Rebounds -128
provenance.writeup: This is a big line for Jonas Valanciunas who is set to see an expanded role with the Nuggets kissing both Nikola Jokic and Aaron Gordon in their frontcourt. Valanciunas hasn’t been playing big minutes and at this stage of his career, a lot would have to go right. In a game that’s projected to be pace down and feature fewer possessions, this looks like a natural slot to fade the lumbering big man.

The Clippers are playing great and Kawhi looks sensational, but this is curious with Nikola Jokic expected back. I'd imagine some minutes limit, but just the emotional boost on top of the fact the dude is the best player in the world. Seriously, you younger ones Google Arvydas Sabonis. He and NK are mirror images in many ways. Why don't American coaches teach this type of big-man offense from the top of the key? Obviously need a rare talent to do it. I'll do a half simply as yeah the Joker might be on a tight cap.

Dillon Brooks is having a career year with Phoenix, scoring 21 points per game and shooting a career best 44.5%. His career has been rebooted and rejuvenated with the best ATS team in the NBA. While Devin Booker is injured, Brooks has scored 26 or more in his last three games and last night scored 40 points. Phoenix is playing a slow-down game, staying under the total in their last four and having Brooks take the most shots without Booker. I don't see the Suns winning tonight, but I do see Brooks getting over 24 points.

The Suns have played the last three games without Devin Booker (ankle) and Jalen Green (hamstring). In all three of those games, Dillon Brooks scored at least 26 points. Thursday, he scored 40 points against a Pistons team that has the second-best defensive rating in the league. It’s also worth noting that all three of those games were played in Phoenix, where Brooks averages 25.1 points per game. Booker will be out again, and even if Green returns, he would likely play limited minutes. Look for Brooks to continue scoring in bunches at home.

Late word from Denver that Nikola Jokic could return tonight...stay tuned. As usual we note Kawhi Leonard's name on the Clippers' daily injury report...but as usual we expect Kawhi to play tonight in Denver. Every game has been important for LA as Ty Lue's bunch has stormed back into the West playoff mix with a stirring 16-3 uptick often fueled by Kawhi, though contributions have been coming from up-and-down the Clips roster. Granted, the Nuggets have hardly disappeared minus Jokic, winning 10 of 16 since the Joker went down, and now he might be back, so proceed carefully, as we posted this play before the Joker news and spread drop. Play Clippers.

FanDuel (alt line -130). Mitchell Robinson, the undisputed best offensive rebounder in the NBA, has cleared this line in 13 of 17 games against teams in the bottom half of opponents offensive rebounds allowed, including each of the last nine. The Trailblazers rank 27th, and Robinson got them for 11 boards earlier in the month. I’d bet this up to over 9.5 at plus odds.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Jock Landale Over 22.5 Total Points + Rebounds -121
provenance.writeup: DraftKings. Jock Landale has cleared this points plus rebounds line in seven of his last ten games, including a season-best 35 PR output against New Orleans last week. The Pelicans allow the fourth most points plus rebounds to opposing centers, and I expect Landale to command most of the workload down low, as Memphis is without Santi Aldama tonight.

Jaylen Brown (hamstring) is listed as doubtful for this game. Payton Pritchard averages 16.7 points and 13.9 shot attempts per game for the season. However, over the three previous games that Brown has missed, Pritchard averaged 24.3 points and 22.3 shot attempts. Additional shot attempts against a Kings team that has the third-worst defensive rating in the league makes this over appealing.

This looks a legit uptick with the Knicks so we just follow our instincts at MSG tonight. There are indicators that the four-game win and cover streak might extend for a bit longer, given the circumstances around its origin, which was the clear-the-air team meeting after a recent loss to Dallas. Mike Brown's troops responded with a 54-point blowout of the Nets and have impressively won and covered the three games since, and not always riding Jalen Brunson; Mikal Bridges led the way with 30 in the Wednesday win at Toronto. Meanwhile a tough road trip for the Blazers who have lost three straight, and while they might have Deni Avdija (back) available tonight, his presence didn't help in Tuesday's loss at Washington. Play Knicks.

I don't believe Luka plays tonight in DC after tweaking his ankle on that weird Cleveland Cavaliers raised court like the University of Minnesota has. Not sure who thought those were a good idea. If Doncic does play, then I'd think he's out of there fairly quickly if this gets out of hand ahead of a marquee game Sunday at the Knicks. I'm definitely pondering Wizards and the points (they have done well for us lately), but that they are in the second of a B2B gives me some pause.

FanDuel (-125). This is really a bet on Justin Champagnie’s minutes, as the Wizards will be without five players due to injury tonight. The wing has cleared this line in 13/19 games with 24+ minutes, and his upside tonight will be in the low 30s. I’d bet this risking one unit at over 10.5.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Chet Holmgren Under 30.5 Total Points + Assists + Rebounds -128
provenance.writeup: FanDuel. Chet Holmgren is averaging 28.2 points plus rebounds plus assists this season. He’s under this line in 12/20 games in regulation when playing with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and without Jalen Williams. The latter subset includes a 23 PRA game against the Timberwolves, who are tough in the interior against big men. The Timberwolves allow the eleventh fewest PRA to opposing power forwards and centers - and Holmgren’s rebounding potential specifically should be affected by Isaiah Hartenstein’s return to the lineup tonight. Chet has not cleared this line in 13 matchups against Minnesota over the last three seasons (regular and postseason). I’d bet this down to under 29.5.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Michael Porter Jr. Over 25.5 Total Points -114
provenance.writeup: Michael Porter is playing in Denver tonight against the team that drafted him, and he started for when they won the NBA Championship. It's going to be a bittersweet moment for Porter, and I think he excels and maybe even leads the Nets to a win. He's averaged 24 points in 18 road games and averages 25.2 points per game this season. The Nets beat the Nuggets in Brooklyn 127-115 on January 4th. Michael Porter had 27 points in that game. Returning home where it all started, I say he gets over 30.

The Suns are going to go it minus Devin Booker (ankle) again tonight, but they've held their own in his absence, including January 15 at Little Caesars when pushing the Pistons to the edge in a 108-105 Detroit escape.  Tuesday's win in the desert over the Nets was indicative of how Phoenix has to do things minus Booker, as Mark Williams (27 points), Dillon Brooks (26 points), and Grayson Allen (18 points) each stepped effectively into the breach.  We have respect for the Pistons but note they rarely have an easy game on the road (such as at Denver two nights ago), and only 6-8 vs. the spread their last 14 away.  Play Suns

The Nets are occasionally competitive, but  the situation is not especially appealing right now in Brooklyn, especially with the trade deadline looming and top scorer Michael Porter, Jr (25.2 ppg) perhaps on the block, while second-leading scorer Cam Thomas might be on the move as well (he's also out tonight).  Many recent Brooklyn  scorelines feature heavy defeats; in the current six-game skid, loss margins have included 22, 54, and 37 points. It's also more than a month since the last road win (December 27), with eight straight away losses. The Nuggets are still minus the Joker but have held their own (9-6) without him, and while Aaron Gordon joins Jokic on the sidelines, Jamal Murray and Peyton Watson are likely available for tonight.  Play Nuggets

Charlotte is playing as well as it has in years, but we are now giving 4.5 on the Hornets in the second of a back-to-back on the road? Let's pump the brakes a little. Granted, all of our other books have 3.5 and it's the second of a B2B for Dallas as well. But it didn't travel. Our model has Bugs by 2. Cool.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Jaime Jaquez Jr. Over 4.5 Total Assists -128
provenance.writeup: Tyler Herro and Davion Mitchell remain out for the Heat, which puts more playmaking responsibility on Jaime Jaquez Jr. In his previous matchup with Chicago, Jaquez dished out seven assists in 24 minutes. He should play nearly 30 minutes Thursday in a game with the highest total on the board. Go Over on assists.

B365/MGM at -120. Ayo Dosunmu has cleared this points line in eleven of his last fourteen games, and in 10/13 games without Josh Giddey this season. With the Bulls also missing Tre Jones tonight, Ayo should be in for a sizable workload off the bench. The Heat run at the league’s fastest pace, which also suits Dosunmu’s skillset. I’d bet this up to 14.5, with the Bulls guard coming off 19 and 20 point games.

DraftKings. Amen Thompson is averaging 18.3 points per game this season. However, when playing against the teams in the bottom half of the NBA of paint points allowed, he’s cleared this line in 17/23 contests, including each of his last 10. The Hawks rank 22nd in paint points allowed, and could be without Onyeka Okongwu tonight, as he left last night’s game early. I’d bet this up to Over 18.5 points.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Pelle Larsson Over 7.5 Total Rebounds + Assists +111
provenance.writeup: Tyler Herro (ribs) and Davion Mitchell (shoulder) have yet to fully practice, so they should be out again Thursday. That will likely continue to leave more playing time for Pelle Larsson. Their recent absences have helped Larsson combine for at least eight rebounds and assists in five of his last six games. This is a great matchup against the Bulls, who allow the fifth-most assists per game in the league. The last time he faced them, Larsson recorded two rebounds and seven assists over 26 minutes. At plus odds, I like taking a chance on this over.

The Bulls continue to play without Tre Jones (hamstring). Ayo Dosunmu has scored at least 19 points in two of three games since Jones suffered his injury. This is the second game of a back-to-back set, and while Josh Giddey and Coby White are expected to play, their recent injuries might still prevent the Bulls from giving them both huge minutes. Dosunmu should log at least 25 minutes in a matchup between two teams that rank inside the top-five in the league in pace of play. He loves to attack the paint, which is a weakness of the Heat because they allow the sixth-most points in the paint per game. The last time he faced them, Dosunmu scored 23 points. Take this over.

The Bucks are a shell of a team at the moment, with Giannis unavailable and apparently on the trading block, and Doc Rivers simply not with enough answers on his roster to do much about it.  The losing streak is now three and six of seven, moreover the defeats have been thumpers; save for a 2-point loss to the Joker-less Nuggets, Milwaukee wasn't closer than 17 points in its other five losses.  Second-leading scorer Kevin Porter, Jr is also out tonight.  Now, do we trust the Wiz?  Their recent efforts have been better than Milwaukee's, finally rewarded with a win against the Blazers two nights ago, and figure to put forth a focused effort on John Wall Appreciation Night at Cap One.  Play Wizards

provenance.raw_pick_text: Alperen Sengun Over 14.5 Total Rebounds + Assists -133
provenance.writeup: Since Houston lost Steven Adams to an ankle sprain, center Alperen Sengun has played four games, clearing this prop total three times. Sengun has faced the Spurs twice this season, finishing with 22 and 16 rebounds plus assists. Sengun says he has put his ankle injury behind him and his play is demonstrating that.

Admittedly, Utah is a pretty hard sell at the moment, with losses in seven of eight...but we suggest the Warriors are a bit of a hard sell, too. Not sure if Lauri Markkanen features tonight for the Jazz after his first action in two weeks on Tuesday vs. the Clippers, when he scored 19, but it looks like Keyonte George (24.2 ppg) will be available after using last night for rest purposes. He's just a week removed from scoring 43 vs. the T-wolves.  Meanwhile, since Jimmy Butler went down, the Dubs have lost 3 of 4, the only win over a distracted Minnesota in the postponed game on Sunday. provenance.raw_pick_text: Jock Landale Over 20.5 Total Points + Rebounds -125
provenance.writeup: DraftKings. As the Grizzlies have cycled players in and out of the rotation, primarily due to injuries, Jock Landale currently finds himself with a significant role. Memphis will be without Santi Aldama and Zach Edey in the frontcourt, and Ja Morant is once again out tonight due to an elbow injury. Even if he comes off the bench, Landale should be featured prominently. Landale has cleared this line in seven of his last eight games he’s played at least 22 minutes. And the matchup against New Orleans is elite - the Pelicans allow the fourth most points plus rebounds to centers this season. I’d bet this up to over 21.5, or at over 23.5 points plus rebounds plus assists.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Jaylon Tyson Over 20.5 Total Points + Rebounds -120
provenance.writeup: FanDuel. Sometimes, you have to just bet the trends - Jaylon Tyson has cleared this line in ten straight games without Darius Garland available. With Sam Merrill also out, Tyson will slide into the starting lineup against an awful defensive Kings team. I’d bet this up to over 21.5.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Dyson Daniels Over 11.5 Total Rebounds + Assists -137
provenance.writeup: Kristaps Porzingis (Achilles) and Zaccharie Risacher (knee) will both be out for the eighth straight game. Dyson Daniels played in six of the previous seven games, averaging 7.8 rebounds and 7.2 assists. He finished with at least 13 combined rebounds and assists in five of the games. The Suns are a good defensive team, but Daniels still produced seven rebounds and 12 assists against them earlier in the season. I like him to be productive against them again.

With word that Cade Cunningham is likely to be a go tonight for the Pistons, less hesitant to back Detroit, which has been overachieving all season and has now won four straight and seven of its last eight. The Pistons continue to handle capable teams like the Celtics, Suns, Cavs, and Lakers in recent weeks, and dealt with Houston's very big lineup in an earlier 115-11 win way back on October 24 at Toyota Center. As for the Rockets, a recent three-game uptick was ended by the Sixers last night, and Houston is only 5-6 across its last eleven games, covering the spread just twice in that stretch.  Play Pistons

This line is in major flux right now because there are a ton of guys in question on either side. But I'm gonna fade Miami regardless on a third game in four nights. We do know that Tyler Herro and Kel'el Ware are out. I've decided to just hammer one NBA game per night and see how that goes as this has been crazy. This is my sport. But also this season is TankFest 101.

FanDuel. Rui Hachimura, who missed extended time with an injury, was worked back to 28 minutes in the Lakers Tuesday win against the Nuggets. Averaging 12 points in 30.7 minutes per game, this line reads as a touch low for Hachimura, even at 25-27 minutes. I’d bet this line up to over 9.5 points at -110 or better.

Rui Hachimura has played limited minutes since returning from injury. However, he has still scored at least nine points in three straight games. What makes me want to take a chance on this over is that he played 28 minutes in his last game, which is approaching his season average of 31 minutes a game. For the season, he has averaged 12.0 points. The Clippers have the ninth-worst defensive rating in the league and Hachimura scored 13 points over 31 minutes against them earlier in the season. I think he plays at least 25 minutes Thursday and maybe even approaches 30 minutes again.

It's wins in three straight and eight of ten for the Blazers, as F Deni Avdija (26.2 ppg) is continuing to perform like a All-Star; after a brief absence, he came back to score 30 in Sunday's 117-110 win at Sacto. For the month, Avdija is scoring at a 30.3 ppg clip in seven games. Meanwhile, Shaedon Sharpe also continues to impress with his consistency, scoring between 19-29 points in eight straight games. As for the Heat, there is question about the the availability of top scorer Norman Powell, who made the injury report this morning with a back issue. Even with Powell, recent form is inconsistent, as the Heat (off a win at Sacto) hasn't won back-to-back since Dec. 29-Jan. 1. Play Blazers

The assumption in Inglewood tonight is that Kawhi Leonard will be available for the Clippers after missing the just-completed three-game road trip. If other news surfaces we'll update this space. With that in mind, there might be something poignant about this particular crosstown matchup as it was the last meeting on December 20 at Crypto.com that lit the fuse for the recent Clippers resurgence. That 103-88 win, with Kawhi scoring 32, triggered a six-game win streak and current 13-3 run that has the Clips back into the West play-in mix. Meanwhile we're not biting on this Lakers mini-uptick of two games, with one of the wins vs. Joker-less Denver and the other vs. a jet-lagged Toronto, preceded by a 1-5 stretch. Play Clippers.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Julius Randle Over 12.5 Total Rebounds + Assists -130
provenance.writeup: This game will be played in Minnesota, where Julius Randle averages 7.1 rebounds and 6.0 assists. This is a great matchup for him against the Bulls, who play at the fourth-fastest pace. That has resulted in them allowing seventh-most rebounds per game and the fifth-most assists per game. When he faced them earlier in the season, Randle finished with five rebounds and 14 assists in Chicago. I like him to remain productive in both departments in this rematch.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Cooper Flagg Over 10.5 Total Rebounds + Assists -114
provenance.writeup: Cooper Flagg has had an extremely impressive rookie campaign while displaying a versatile skillset and on court maturity few rookies possess. I like isolating his RA line considering how much the ball is in his hands and also because his playmaking has really begun to develop. In what projects to be a competitive game he possesses double digit upside in both categories and I have a significant edge on my projections here as well.

provenance.raw_pick_text: PJ Washington Under 24.5 Total Points + Assists + Rebounds -118
provenance.writeup: This is a big combo line for Washington who has been largely absent from the Dallas lineup in the month of January. Dallas has no shortage of capable scorers and high usage players so it’s not a given that Washington is going to get a lot of shots here either.

FanDuel. Joel Embiid has cleared this line in nine of his last 12 games. Fully off his minutes restrictions, I see this matchup as a plus spot for the big man to produce. The Rockets have struggled a bit defending high usage big men, and I expect that to continue without Steven Adams available. The duo of Alperen Sengun and Clint Capela underwhelms defensively, and I don’t expect the Rockets to abandon their main defensive scheme and double team the big man.

DraftKings. Anthony Black has been a revelation for the Magic, who’ve dealt with a litany of injuries this season. With Franz Wagner and Jalen Suggs out tonight, Black should assume a sizable role as a starter for Orlando. Black has cleared this line in 12 of 14 games without Wagner and Suggs, registering a 26% usage rate in those contests. The Hornets defense on the perimeter is nothing to write home about - Black has 20 and 24 points against them this season. I’d bet this up to over 18.5 points, but hopping on this line at DraftKings.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Amen Thompson Over 12.5 Total Rebounds + Assists -127
provenance.writeup: Amen Thompson is playing a career high 37 minutes per game. That has helped him average 7.7 rebounds and 5.1 assists. On the road, he is averaging 8.5 rebounds and 5.5 assists. Steven Adams (ankle) being out means that the Rockets will be without one of their best rebounders. In the nine games that Adams has missed this season, Thompson has averaged 13.8 combined rebounds and assists. The 76ers rank 20th in rebounds allowed and 23rd in assists allowed per game, which makes this over even more appealing for Thompson.

This looks like a good spot to back DeMar DeRozan who will be facing a familiar opponent in the Toronto Raptors tonight. DeRozan has eclipsed this line in his previous six head to head matchups against the Raptors. I also like that DeRozan has been playing well recently averaging over 20 PPG in the month of January. This game projects to be competitive and I expect DeRozan to play 35+ minutes.

The Kings might be the worst-run organization in the NBA, but they appear to have hit on second-round rookie Maxime Raynaud. He should see extended minutes tonight in the second of a B2B with Domantas Sabonis out and fellow big Precious Achiuwa in doubt. Toronto is missing its two top big men, so there should be plenty of rebounds to be had.

It all fell nicely into place for the Raptors last night at Golden State, as the Warriors were flat minus Jimmy Butler and never had a look at a 145-127 romp for Toronto. 
Sacto is also off of  a game last night at Golden 1 Center when the Heat shot out the lights ina 130-117 win, and Domantas Sabonis, who recently returned to active duty, will miss tonight for load management.  But the Kings had been winning minus Sabonis in the early past of this home stand when they won four straight, including vs. the Knicks, Lakers, and Rockets.  The Raps had lost four of six before catching the Dubs vulnerable last night, and the quick-reversal dynamics favor the Kings tonight.  Play Kings

I like this spot to fade Giannis against an elite Oklahoma Thunder team who are double digit favorites. Giannis also hasn’t been playing big minutes and is averaging under 30 MPG in the month of January. Considering the blowout potential, matchup, and Giannis recent playing time, this looks like a natural fade of the former MVP.

Fading the Thunder has not proven wise for the most part, but man 12.5 in Milwaukee with Giannis in the lineup. And OKC is pretty thinned-out due to injury down Alex Caruso, Isaiah Hartenstein, Andrew Wiggins and the Williamses. If the Thunder can still win by 13 at the end of a four-game trip, good for them.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Isaiah Joe Over 2.5 Total 3pt Field Goals -106
provenance.writeup: FanDuel. It’s tough to pinpoint exactly which of the Thunder’s endless supply role players will pop, on any given night. But tonight, the options are narrowed, with Jalen Williams, Alex Caruso, Aaron Wiggins, Isaiah Hartenstein and Jaylin Williams all out. Enter Isaiah Joe, who is shooting 41% from long range, with 2.3 makes in 19.4 minutes per game this season. The sharpshooter should be in for added minutes tonight in a great matchup. The Bucks allow the seventh most catch and shoot opportunities per game, at the sixth highest frequency. Joe has cleared this line in 10/13 games when he’s played just 22 minutes, and 10/15 overall without Jalen Williams.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Dyson Daniels Over 12.5 Total Rebounds + Assists -119
provenance.writeup: Kristaps Porzingis (Achilles) and Zaccharie Risacher (knee) will miss their seventh straight game for the Hawks. Dyson Daniels played in five of the last six games without them, averaging 8.0 rebounds and 7.0 assists. Going back even further, Daniels has posted at least 13 combined rebounds and assists in 13 of his last 18 games. The Hawks and Grizzlies both rank inside the top 11 in the league in pace of play, so Daniels could have ample opportunities to reach this over.

Though Ja Morant's presence might be a distraction, he can still make positive contributions, as when returning to active duty on Sunday in London, scoring 24 in a 126-109 Griz win over the Magic to split the brief European tour with Orlando. Back home, Memphis, with all of its turbulence, still sits on the edge of the West play-in field, and has been mostly competitive lately with 6-10 Jarren Jackson Jr making consistent contributions and some highlight-reel efforts from Washington State rookie wing Cedric Coward, who scored 21 in the last Griz home game (a win over the Nets on January 11). Meanwhile recently-added CJ McCollum has yet to shoot 50% from the floor in any of his four Hawks games, all losses. Play Grizzlies

provenance.raw_pick_text: Luka Garza Over 13.5 Total Points + Rebounds -107
provenance.writeup: DraftKings. I’m on Luka Garza again tonight, as this reads as a plus matchup. Garza is over this line in 17/23 games this season when playing at least 14 minutes, including 2/3 against the Pacers. His minutes have waned a bit over the last two games, but that was a product of foul trouble and gamescript. The big man gets a fantastic matchup against the Pacers, a bottom six team against all of Garza’s main scoring types.

As long as Jaylen Brown is good to go  (which seems likely, listed as probable on today's injury report), the revenge angle for the Celtics is worth strong consideration at TD Garden. Last Monday, Boston was caught flat at Indy, when it also played without the high-scoring Brown and lost a 98-96 squeaker vs. the Pacers, who were on a brief (and rare) uptick at that moment, winning their third straight for the first time this season. The Pacers, however, remain shorthanded, and have lost three of four since, including a lopsided 43-point beating administered by the Pistons on Saturday. The last time Brown was available vs. Indiana was December 26 at Gainbridge Fieldhouse, when he scored 30 and the Celtics rolled, 140-122. Play Celtics

provenance.raw_pick_text: Miles Bridges Over 8.5 Total Rebounds + Assists -143
provenance.writeup: Miles Bridges averages 6.4 rebounds and 3.6 assists per game for the season. The Cavaliers have a big frontcourt of Jarrett Allen and Evan Mobley, but they rank 17th in the league in rebounds allowed per game. They also rank 20th in assists allowed per game. In two previous meetings with the Cavaliers this season, Bridges recorded 16 and 10 combined rebounds and assists. There is plenty of juice with this over, but I still like it.

Understandably, Donovan Mitchell draws  lots of attention on the Cleveland side, but almost as valuable to the Cavs cause is PG Darius Garland (18 ppg), whose absence due to a big toe sprain is hardly insignificant, and was felt by Cleveland in the heavy 32-point loss on Monday vs. OKC.
Meanwhile, the Hornets have most hands on deck and have flashed plenty of KO power, recently putting 150 on Utah, 135 on the Lakers, and winning by 23 vs. the Nuggets...all on the road.  Now Charlotte looks to break a puzzling four-game skid at the Bee Hive, but has often been hitting on all cylinders, with Brandon Miller and LaMelo Ball the sharpest edges in recent wins over LA and Denver.  Play Hornets

Russ Westbrook has played like an athlete that has turned back the clock recently. Over his last four games he has scored 19 points or more. His play has been a big factor in the Kings being 4-1 over their last five games. Look for a drop in production as Westbrook is exceeding his capabilities especially from beyond the arc, where he has made 9 of his last 15. Tail his under tonight.

provenance.raw_pick_text: Jaime Jaquez Jr. Over 22.5 Total Points + Assists + Rebounds -120
provenance.writeup: B365/MGM. Jaime Jaquez is averaging 25.6 combined points, rebounds and assists this season, but that number jumps to 27.5 without Tyler Herro. In full games without Herro, Jaquez has cleared this line in 22/30 games, including a 39 PRA effort against the Kings earlier this season. Against Sacramento’s bottom barrel defense (27th), look for Jaquez to once again shine. I’d bet this up to over 23.5.
